revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: ''''丸隈山古墳'''（まるくまやまこふん）は、[[福岡県]][[福岡市]]西区周船寺の[[高祖山]]のふもとに位置する前方後円墳で…'
revision.text:
'''丸隈山古墳'''（まるくまやまこふん）は、[[福岡県]][[福岡市]]西区周船寺の[[高祖山]]のふもとに位置する前方後円墳である。
== 概要 == 全長84.6ｍ、後円部の直径60ｍ、前方部25ｍ。5世紀前半の首長級の墳墓と推定されている。
玄武岩で作られた横穴式石室は無料公開されており、見学者が石棺を直接見ることができる数少ない国宝指定古墳である。
江戸時代の「筑前国続風土記」で[[貝原益軒]]は、ここから銅鏡、刀、鉄鏃などが出土したと記録している。 その他に巴形銅器、剣、勾玉、管玉なども出土した。埋葬人骨は男性のものとされている。

'''丸隈山古墳'''（まるくまやまこふん）は、[[福岡県]][[福岡市]]西区周船寺の[[高祖山]]のふもとに位置する前方後円墳である。
== 概要 == 全長84.6ｍ、後円部の直径60ｍ、前方部25ｍ。5世紀前半の首長級の墳墓と推定されている。
玄武岩で作られた横穴式石室は無料公開されており、見学者が石棺を直接見ることができる数少ない国宝指定古墳である。
江戸時代の「筑前国続風土記」で[[貝原益軒]]は、ここから銅鏡、刀、鉄鏃などが出土したと記録している。 その他に巴形銅器、剣、勾玉、管玉なども出土した。埋葬人骨は男性のものとされている。 {{Japanese-history-stub}}

江戸時代の「筑前国続風土記」で[[貝原益軒]]は、ここから銅鏡、刀、鉄鏃などが出土したと記録している。 その他に巴形銅器、剣、勾玉、管玉なども出土した。埋葬人骨は男性のものとされている。
{{DEFAULTSORT:まるくまやまこふん}} [[Category:福岡県の古墳]] [[Category:福岡県にある国指定の史跡]] {{Japanese-history-stub}}

{{日本の古墳 |名称 =丸隈山古墳 |画像 ={{画像募集中|cat=福岡市}} |画像キャプション = |別名 = |所属 = [[今宿古墳群]] |所在地 =[[福岡県]][[福岡市]][[西区 (福岡市)|西区]][[周船寺]] |位置 = |緯度度 =33 |緯度分 =34 |緯度秒 =25.3 |経度度 =130 |経度分 =15 |経度秒 =7.1 |ISO =JP-40 |形状 =[[前方後円墳]] |規模 =全長84.6m、後円部直径60m、前方部25m |埋葬施設 = |出土品 =銅鏡、刀、鉄鏃など |陪塚 = |築造時期 =[[5世紀]]前半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 =国指定史跡 |有形文化財 = |埋蔵文化財包蔵地番号 = |特記事項 = |地図 =Japan Fukuoka |地図2 = |アイコン = |ラベル = |ラベル位置 = }} '''丸隈山古墳'''（まるくまやまこふん）は、[[福岡県]][[福岡市]][[西区 (福岡市)|西区]][[周船寺]]の[[高祖山]]のふもとに位置する[[前方後円墳]]である。「[[今宿古墳群]]」のうちの1基として国の[[史跡]]に指定されている。
== 規模等 == 全長84.6m、後円部の直径60m、前方部25m。5世紀前半の首長級の墳墓と推定されている。玄武岩で作られた横穴式石室と内部の石棺は無料公開されている。国の史跡に指定されている古墳のうち、見学者が石棺を直接見ることができる数少ないものの1つである。江戸時代の「[[筑前国続風土記]]」で[[貝原益軒]]は、ここから銅鏡、刀、鉄鏃などが出土したと記録している。 その他に巴形銅器、剣、勾玉、管玉なども出土した。埋葬人骨は男性のものとされている。
== 外部リンク == *{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|2614|今宿古墳群}} *[https://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/cultural_properties/detail/item_id:101009 今宿古墳群（丸隈山古墳）] - 福岡市の文化財 *[https://www.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/nishiku/c-shinko/charm/nisikunotakara/marukumayama_kofun.html 丸隈山古墳] - 福岡市ホームページ

revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 =丸隈山古墳 |画像 ={{画像募集中|cat=福岡市}} |画像キャプション = |別名 = |所属 = {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|ja|今宿古墳群}} |所在地 ={{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}} {{ill|Fukuoka City|ja|福岡市}} {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|ja|西区}} {{ill|Susenji, Fukuoka|ja|周船寺}} |位置 = |緯度度 =33 |緯度分 =34 |緯度秒 =25.3 |経度度 =130 |経度分 =15 |経度秒 =7.1 |ISO =JP-40 |形状 ={{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} |規模 =Total length 84.6 m, rear circular section diameter 60 m, front section 25 m |埋葬施設 = |出土品 =Bronze mirrors, swords, iron arrowheads, etc. |陪塚 = |築造時期 =Early 5th century |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 =Designated as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] |有形文化財 = |埋蔵文化財包蔵地番号 = |特記事項 = |地図 =Japan Fukuoka |地図2 = |アイコン = |ラベル = |ラベル位置 = }} '''Marukumayama Kofun''' (丸隈山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} located at the foot of {{ill|Takasu Mountain|ja|高祖山}} in {{ill|Susenji, Fukuoka|ja|周船寺}}, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|ja|西区}}, {{ill|Fukuoka City|ja|福岡市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}. It is designated as one of the key tumuli in the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|ja|今宿古墳群}}, which has been recognized as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].
== Scale and Features == The tumulus measures 84.6 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 60 meters and the front section measuring 25 meters. It is estimated to be the burial site of a chieftain from the early 5th century.
The kofun features a {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室}} made of basalt and an internal stone sarcophagus, which are open to public viewing at no cost. It is one of the few nationally designated tumuli where visitors can directly see the stone sarcophagus. {{Infobox ancient site |name =丸隈山古墳 |image ={{image募集中|cat=福岡市}} |imageキャプション = |別名 = |part_of = {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|ja|今宿古墳群}} |所在地 ={{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}} {{ill|Fukuoka City|ja|福岡市}} {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|ja|西区}} {{ill|Susenji, Fukuoka|ja|周船寺}} |coordinates = |緯度度 =33 |緯度分 =34 |緯度秒 =25.3 |経度度 =130 |経度分 =15 |経度秒 =7.1 |ISO =JP-40 |形状 ={{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} |規模 =Total length 84.6 m, rear circular section diameter 60 m, front section 25 m |埋葬施設 = |出土品 =Bronze mirrors, swords, iron arrowheads, etc. |陪塚 = |築造時期 =Early 5th century |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 =Designated as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] |有形文化財 = |埋蔵文化財包蔵地番号 = |特記事項 = |地図 =Japan Fukuoka |地図2 = |アイコン = |ラベル = |ラベルcoordinates = }} '''Marukumayama Kofun''' (丸隈山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} located at the foot of {{ill|Takasu Mountain|ja|高祖山}} in {{ill|Susenji, Fukuoka|ja|周船寺}}, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|ja|西区}}, {{ill|Fukuoka City|ja|福岡市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}. It is designated as one of the key tumuli in the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|ja|今宿古墳群}}, which has been recognized as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].
== Scale and Features == The tumulus measures 84.6 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 60 meters and the front section measuring 25 meters. It is estimated to be the burial site of a chieftain from the early 5th century.
The kofun features a {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室}} made of basalt and an internal stone sarcophagus, which are open to public viewing at no cost. It is one of the few nationally designated tumuli where visitors can directly see the stone sarcophagus. According to the Edo period document {{ill|Chikuzen Koku Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記}} by {{ill|Kaibara Ekken|ja|貝原益軒}}, artifacts such as bronze mirrors, swords, and iron arrowheads were excavated from the site.
Other finds include tomoe-shaped bronze implements, swords, magatama beads, and tubular beads. The human remains discovered in the tomb are believed to belong to a male.

It is designated as one of the key tumuli in the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|ja|今宿古墳群}}, which has been recognized as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].
== Scale and Features == The tumulus measures 84.6 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 60 meters and the front section measuring 25 meters. It is estimated to be the burial site of a chieftain from the early 5th century.
The kofun features a {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室}} made of basalt and an internal stone sarcophagus, which are open to public viewing at no cost. It is one of the few nationally designated tumuli where visitors can directly see the stone sarcophagus. According to the Edo period document {{ill|Chikuzen Koku Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記}} by {{ill|Kaibara Ekken|ja|貝原益軒}}, artifacts such as bronze mirrors, swords, and iron arrowheads were excavated from the site.
Other finds include tomoe-shaped bronze implements, swords, magatama beads, and tubular beads. The human remains discovered in the tomb are believed to belong to a male.

It is designated as one of the key tumuli in the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|ja|今宿古墳群}}, which has been recognized as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].
== Scale and Features == The tumulus measures 84.6 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 60 meters and the front section measuring 25 meters. It is estimated to be the burial site of a chieftain from the early 5th century.
The kofun features a {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} made of basalt and an internal stone sarcophagus, which are open to public viewing at no cost. It is one of the few nationally designated tumuli where visitors can directly see the stone sarcophagus. According to the Edo period document {{ill|Chikuzen Koku Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記}} by {{ill|Kaibara Ekken|ja|貝原益軒}}, artifacts such as bronze mirrors, swords, and iron arrowheads were excavated from the site.
Other finds include tomoe-shaped bronze implements, swords, magatama beads, and tubular beads. The human remains discovered in the tomb are believed to belong to a male.

{{Infobox ancient site |name =丸隈山古墳 |image ={{image募集中|cat=福岡市}} |imageキャプション = |別名 = |part_of = {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|ja|今宿古墳群}} |所在地 ={{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}} {{ill|Fukuoka City|ja|福岡市}} {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|ja|西区}} {{ill|Susenji, Fukuoka|ja|周船寺}} |coordinates = |緯度度 =33 |緯度分 =34 |緯度秒 =25.3 |経度度 =130 |経度分 =15 |経度秒 =7.1 |ISO =JP-40 |形状 ={{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |規模 =Total length 84.6 m, rear circular section diameter 60 m, front section 25 m |埋葬施設 = |出土品 =Bronze mirrors, swords, iron arrowheads, etc. |陪塚 = |築造時期 =Early 5th century |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 =Designated as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] |有形文化財 = |埋蔵文化財包蔵地番号 = |特記事項 = |地図 =Japan Fukuoka |地図2 = |アイコン = |ラベル = |ラベルcoordinates = }} '''Marukumayama Kofun''' (丸隈山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located at the foot of {{ill|Takasu Mountain|ja|高祖山}} in {{ill|Susenji, Fukuoka|ja|周船寺}}, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|ja|西区}}, {{ill|Fukuoka City|ja|福岡市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}. It is designated as one of the key tumuli in the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|ja|今宿古墳群}}, which has been recognized as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].
== Scale and Features == The tumulus measures 84.6 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 60 meters and the front section measuring 25 meters. It is estimated to be the burial site of a chieftain from the early 5th century.
The kofun features a {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} made of basalt and an internal stone sarcophagus, which are open to public viewing at no cost. It is one of the few nationally designated tumuli where visitors can directly see the stone sarcophagus. According to the Edo period document {{ill|Chikuzen Koku Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記}} by {{ill|Kaibara Ekken|ja|貝原益軒}}, artifacts such as bronze mirrors, swords, and iron arrowheads were excavated from the site.
Other finds include tomoe-shaped bronze implements, swords, magatama beads, and tubular beads. The human remains discovered in the tomb are believed to belong to a male.
== See Also == * {{ill|Yamanohane No. 1 Kofun|ja|山ノ鼻1号墳}}

{{Infobox ancient site |name =丸隈山古墳 |image ={{image募集中|cat=福岡市}} |imageキャプション = |別名 = |part_of = {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|ja|今宿古墳群}} |所在地 ={{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}} {{ill|Fukuoka City|ja|福岡市}} {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|ja|西区}} {{ill|Susenji, Fukuoka|ja|周船寺}} |coordinates = |緯度度 =33 |緯度分 =34 |緯度秒 =25.3 |経度度 =130 |経度分 =15 |経度秒 =7.1 |ISO =JP-40 |形状 ={{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |規模 =Total length 84.6 m, rear circular section diameter 60 m, front section 25 m |埋葬施設 = |出土品 =Bronze mirrors, swords, iron arrowheads, etc. |陪塚 = |築造時期 =Early 5th century |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 =Designated as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] |有形文化財 = |埋蔵文化財包蔵地番号 = |特記事項 = |地図 =Japan Fukuoka |地図2 = |アイコン = |ラベル = |ラベルcoordinates = }} '''Marukumayama Kofun''' (丸隈山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located at the foot of {{ill|Takasu Mountain|ja|高祖山}} in {{ill|Susenji, Fukuoka|ja|周船寺}}, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|ja|西区}}, {{ill|Fukuoka City|ja|福岡市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}. It is designated as one of the key tumuli in the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|ja|今宿古墳群}}, which has been recognized as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].
== Scale and Features == The tumulus measures 84.6 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 60 meters and the front section measuring 25 meters. It is estimated to be the burial site of a chieftain from the early 5th century.
The kofun features a {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} made of basalt and an internal stone sarcophagus, which are open to public viewing at no cost. It is one of the few nationally designated tumuli where visitors can directly see the stone sarcophagus. According to the Edo period document {{ill|Chikuzen Koku Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記}} by {{ill|Kaibara Ekken|ja|貝原益軒}}, artifacts such as bronze mirrors, swords, and iron arrowheads were excavated from the site.
Other finds include tomoe-shaped bronze implements, swords, magatama beads, and tubular beads. The human remains discovered in the tomb are believed to belong to a male.
== See Also == * {{ill|Yamanohane No. 1 Kofun|ja|山ノ鼻1号墳}}

{{Infobox ancient site |name =丸隈山古墳 |image ={{image募集中|cat=福岡市}} |imageキャプション = |別名 = |part_of = {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|ja|今宿古墳群}} |所在地 ={{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}} {{ill|Fukuoka City|ja|福岡市}} {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|ja|西区}} {{ill|Susenji, Fukuoka|ja|周船寺}} |coordinates = |緯度度 =33 |緯度分 =34 |緯度秒 =25.3 |経度度 =130 |経度分 =15 |経度秒 =7.1 |ISO =JP-40 |形状 ={{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |規模 =Total length 84.6 m, rear circular section diameter 60 m, front section 25 m |埋葬施設 = |出土品 =Bronze mirrors, swords, iron arrowheads, etc. |陪塚 = |築造時期 =Early 5th century |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 =Designated as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] |有形文化財 = |埋蔵文化財包蔵地番号 = |特記事項 = |地図 =Japan Fukuoka |地図2 = |アイコン = |ラベル = |ラベルcoordinates = }} '''Marukumayama Kofun''' (丸隈山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located at the foot of {{ill|Takasu Mountain|ja|高祖山}} in {{ill|Susenji, Fukuoka|ja|周船寺}}, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|ja|西区}}, {{ill|Fukuoka City|ja|福岡市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}. It is designated as one of the key tumuli in the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|ja|今宿古墳群}}, which has been recognized as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].
== Scale and Features == The tumulus measures 84.6 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 60 meters and the front section measuring 25 meters. It is estimated to be the burial site of a chieftain from the early 5th century.
The kofun features a {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} made of basalt and an internal stone sarcophagus, which are open to public viewing at no cost. It is one of the few nationally designated tumuli where visitors can directly see the stone sarcophagus. According to the Edo period document {{ill|Chikuzen Koku Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記}} by {{ill|Kaibara Ekken|ja|貝原益軒}}, artifacts such as bronze mirrors, swords, and iron arrowheads were excavated from the site.
Other finds include tomoe-shaped bronze implements, swords, magatama beads, and tubular beads. The human remains discovered in the tomb are believed to belong to a male.
== See Also == * {{ill|Yamanohane No. 1 Kofun|ja|山ノ鼻1号墳}}

{{Infobox ancient site |name =丸隈山古墳 |image ={{image募集中|cat=福岡市}} |imageキャプション = |別名 = |part_of = {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|ja|今宿古墳群}} |所在地 ={{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}} {{ill|Fukuoka City|ja|福岡市}} {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|ja|西区}} {{ill|Susenji, Fukuoka|ja|周船寺}} |coordinates = |緯度度 =33 |緯度分 =34 |緯度秒 =25.3 |経度度 =130 |経度分 =15 |経度秒 =7.1 |ISO =JP-40 |形状 ={{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |規模 =Total length 84.6 m, rear circular section diameter 60 m, front section 25 m |埋葬施設 = |出土品 =Bronze mirrors, swords, iron arrowheads, etc. |陪塚 = |築造時期 =Early 5th century |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 =Designated as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] |有形文化財 = |埋蔵文化財包蔵地番号 = |特記事項 = |地図 =Japan Fukuoka |地図2 = |アイコン = |ラベル = |ラベルcoordinates = }} '''Marukumayama Kofun''' (丸隈山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located at the foot of {{ill|Takasu Mountain|ja|高祖山}} in {{ill|Susenji, Fukuoka|ja|周船寺}}, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|ja|西区}}, {{ill|Fukuoka City|ja|福岡市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}. It is designated as one of the key tumuli in the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|ja|今宿古墳群}}, which has been recognized as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].
== Scale and Features == The tumulus measures 84.6 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 60 meters and the front section measuring 25 meters. It is estimated to be the burial site of a chieftain from the early 5th century.
The kofun features a {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} made of basalt and an internal stone sarcophagus, which are open to public viewing at no cost. It is one of the few nationally designated tumuli where visitors can directly see the stone sarcophagus. According to the Edo period document {{ill|Chikuzen Koku Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記}} by {{ill|Kaibara Ekken|ja|貝原益軒}}, artifacts such as bronze mirrors, swords, and iron arrowheads were excavated from the site.
Other finds include tomoe-shaped bronze implements, swords, magatama beads, and tubular beads. The human remains discovered in the tomb are believed to belong to a male.
== See Also == * {{ill|Yamanohane No. 1 Kofun|ja|山ノ鼻1号墳}}

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name =丸隈山古墳 |image ={{image募集中|cat=福岡市}} |imageキャプション = |別名 = |part_of = {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|ja|今宿古墳群}} |所在地 ={{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}} {{ill|Fukuoka City|ja|福岡市}} {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|ja|西区}} {{ill|Susenji, Fukuoka|ja|周船寺}} |coordinates = |緯度度 =33 |緯度分 =34 |緯度秒 =25.3 |経度度 =130 |経度分 =15 |経度秒 =7.1 |ISO =JP-40 |形状 ={{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |規模 =Total length 84.6 m, rear circular section diameter 60 m, front section 25 m |埋葬施設 = |出土品 =Bronze mirrors, swords, iron arrowheads, etc. |陪塚 = |築造時期 =Early 5th century |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 =Designated as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] |有形文化財 = |埋蔵文化財包蔵地番号 = |特記事項 = |地図 =Japan Fukuoka |地図2 = |アイコン = |ラベル = |ラベルcoordinates = }} '''Marukumayama Kofun''' (丸隈山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located at the foot of {{ill|Takasu Mountain|ja|高祖山}} in {{ill|Susenji, Fukuoka|ja|周船寺}}, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|ja|西区}}, {{ill|Fukuoka City|ja|福岡市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}. It is designated as one of the key tumuli in the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|ja|今宿古墳群}}, which has been recognized as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].
== Scale and Features == The tumulus measures 84.6 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 60 meters and the front section measuring 25 meters. It is estimated to be the burial site of a chieftain from the early 5th century.
The kofun features a {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} made of basalt and an internal stone sarcophagus, which are open to public viewing at no cost. It is one of the few nationally designated tumuli where visitors can directly see the stone sarcophagus. According to the Edo period document {{ill|Chikuzen Koku Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記}} by {{ill|Kaibara Ekken|ja|貝原益軒}}, artifacts such as bronze mirrors, swords, and iron arrowheads were excavated from the site.
Other finds include tomoe-shaped bronze implements, swords, magatama beads, and tubular beads. The human remains discovered in the tomb are believed to belong to a male.
== See Also == * {{ill|Yamanohane No. 1 Kofun|ja|山ノ鼻1号墳}}
== External Links == * {{CulturalPropertyDatabase|401|2614|Imajuku Kofun Cluster}} * [https://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/cultural_properties/detail/item_id:101009 Imajuku Kofun Cluster (Marukumayama Kofun)] - Fukuoka City Cultural Heritage * [https://www.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/nishiku/c-shinko/charm/nisikunotakara/marukumayama_kofun.html Marukumayama Kofun] - Fukuoka City Homepage

{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name =丸隈山古墳 |image ={{image募集中|cat=福岡市}} |imageキャプション = |別名 = |part_of = {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|ja|今宿古墳群}} |所在地 ={{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}} {{ill|Fukuoka City|ja|福岡市}} {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|ja|西区}} {{ill|Susenji, Fukuoka|ja|周船寺}} |coordinates = |緯度度 =33 |緯度分 =34 |緯度秒 =25.3 |経度度 =130 |経度分 =15 |経度秒 =7.1 |ISO =JP-40 |形状 ={{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |規模 =Total length 84.6 m, rear circular section diameter 60 m, front section 25 m |埋葬施設 = |出土品 =Bronze mirrors, swords, iron arrowheads, etc. |陪塚 = |築造時期 =Early 5th century |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 =Designated as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] |有形文化財 = |埋蔵文化財包蔵地番号 = |特記事項 = |地図 =Japan Fukuoka |地図2 = |アイコン = |ラベル = |ラベルcoordinates = }} '''Marukumayama Kofun''' (丸隈山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located at the foot of {{ill|Takasu Mountain|ja|高祖山}} in {{ill|Susenji, Fukuoka|ja|周船寺}}, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|ja|西区}}, {{ill|Fukuoka City|ja|福岡市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}. It is designated as one of the key tumuli in the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|ja|今宿古墳群}}, which has been recognized as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].
== Scale and Features == The tumulus measures 84.6 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 60 meters and the front section measuring 25 meters. It is estimated to be the burial site of a chieftain from the early 5th century.
The kofun features a {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} made of basalt and an internal stone sarcophagus, which are open to public viewing at no cost. It is one of the few nationally designated tumuli where visitors can directly see the stone sarcophagus. According to the Edo period document {{ill|Chikuzen Koku Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記}} by {{ill|Kaibara Ekken|ja|貝原益軒}}, artifacts such as bronze mirrors, swords, and iron arrowheads were excavated from the site.
Other finds include tomoe-shaped bronze implements, swords, magatama beads, and tubular beads. The human remains discovered in the tomb are believed to belong to a male.
== See Also == * {{ill|Yamanohane No. 1 Kofun|ja|山ノ鼻1号墳}}
== External Links == * {{CulturalPropertyDatabase|401|2614|Imajuku Kofun Cluster}} * [https://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/cultural_properties/detail/item_id:101009 Imajuku Kofun Cluster (Marukumayama Kofun)] - Fukuoka City Cultural Heritage * [https://www.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/nishiku/c-shinko/charm/nisikunotakara/marukumayama_kofun.html Marukumayama Kofun] - Fukuoka City Homepage

Other finds include tomoe-shaped bronze implements, swords, magatama beads, and tubular beads. The human remains discovered in the tomb are believed to belong to a male.

revision.comment: Text replacement - "there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure" to "there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name =丸隈山古墳 |image ={{image募集中|cat=福岡市}} |imageキャプション = |別名 = |part_of = {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|ja|今宿古墳群}} |所在地 ={{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}} {{ill|Fukuoka City|ja|福岡市}} {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|ja|西区}} {{ill|Susenji, Fukuoka|ja|周船寺}} |coordinates = |緯度度 =33 |緯度分 =34 |緯度秒 =25.3 |経度度 =130 |経度分 =15 |経度秒 =7.1 |ISO =JP-40 |type ={{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |規模 =Total length 84.6 m, rear circular section diameter 60 m, front section 25 m |埋葬施設 = |burial goods =Bronze mirrors, swords, iron arrowheads, etc. |陪塚 = |築造時期 =Early 5th century |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 =Designated as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] |有形文化財 = |埋蔵文化財包蔵地番号 = |特記事項 = |地図 =Japan Fukuoka |地図2 = |アイコン = |ラベル = |ラベルcoordinates = }} '''Marukumayama Kofun''' (丸隈山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located at the foot of {{ill|Takasu Mountain|ja|高祖山}} in {{ill|Susenji, Fukuoka|ja|周船寺}}, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|ja|西区}}, {{ill|Fukuoka City|ja|福岡市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}. It is designated as one of the key tumuli in the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|ja|今宿古墳群}}, which has been recognized as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].
== Scale and Features == The tumulus measures 84.6 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 60 meters and the front section measuring 25 meters. It is estimated to be the burial site of a chieftain from the early 5th century.
The kofun features a {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} made of basalt and an internal stone sarcophagus, which are open to public viewing at no cost. It is one of the few nationally designated tumuli where visitors can directly see the stone sarcophagus. According to the Edo period document {{ill|Chikuzen Koku Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記}} by {{ill|Kaibara Ekken|ja|貝原益軒}}, artifacts such as bronze mirrors, swords, and iron arrowheads were excavated from the site.
Other finds include tomoe-shaped bronze implements, swords, magatama beads, and tubular beads. The human remains discovered in the tomb are believed to belong to a male.== See Also == * {{ill|Yamanohane No. 1 Kofun|ja|山ノ鼻1号墳}}
== External Links == * {{CulturalPropertyDatabase|401|2614|Imajuku Kofun Cluster}} * [https://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/cultural_properties/detail/item_id:101009 Imajuku Kofun Cluster (Marukumayama Kofun)] - Fukuoka City Cultural Heritage * [https://www.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/nishiku/c-shinko/charm/nisikunotakara/marukumayama_kofun.html Marukumayama Kofun] - Fukuoka City Homepage

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name =丸隈山古墳 |image ={{image募集中|cat=福岡市}} |imageキャプション = |別名 = |part_of = {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|ja|今宿古墳群}} |所在地 ={{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}} {{ill|Fukuoka City|ja|福岡市}} {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|ja|西区}} {{ill|Susenji, Fukuoka|ja|周船寺}} |coordinates = |緯度度 =33 |緯度分 =34 |緯度秒 =25.3 |経度度 =130 |経度分 =15 |経度秒 =7.1 |ISO =JP-40 |type ={{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |規模 =Total length 84.6 m, rear circular section diameter 60 m, front section 25 m |埋葬施設 = |burial goods =Bronze mirrors, swords, iron arrowheads, etc. |陪塚 = |築造時期 =Early 5th century |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 =Designated as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] |有形文化財 = |埋蔵文化財包蔵地番号 = |特記事項 = |地図 =Japan Fukuoka |地図2 = |アイコン = |ラベル = |ラベルcoordinates = }} '''Marukumayama Kofun''' (丸隈山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located at the foot of {{ill|Takasu Mountain|ja|高祖山}} in {{ill|Susenji, Fukuoka|ja|周船寺}}, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|ja|西区}}, {{ill|Fukuoka City|ja|福岡市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}. It is designated as one of the key tumuli in the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|ja|今宿古墳群}}, which has been recognized as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].
== map_size and Features == The tumulus measures 84.6 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 60 meters and the front section measuring 25 meters. It is estimated to be the burial site of a chieftain from the early 5th century.
The kofun features a {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} made of basalt and an internal stone sarcophagus, which are open to public viewing at no cost. It is one of the few nationally designated tumuli where visitors can directly see the stone sarcophagus. According to the Edo period document {{ill|Chikuzen Koku Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記}} by {{ill|Kaibara Ekken|ja|貝原益軒}}, artifacts such as bronze mirrors, swords, and iron arrowheads were excavated from the site.
Other finds include tomoe-shaped bronze implements, swords, magatama beads, and tubular beads. The human remains discovered in the tomb are believed to belong to a male.== See Also == * {{ill|Yamanohane No. 1 Kofun|ja|山ノ鼻1号墳}}

The kofun features a {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} made of basalt and an internal stone sarcophagus, which are open to public viewing at no cost. It is one of the few nationally designated tumuli where visitors can directly see the stone sarcophagus. According to the Edo period document {{ill|Chikuzen Koku Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記}} by {{ill|Kaibara Ekken|ja|貝原益軒}}, artifacts such as bronze mirrors, swords, and iron arrowheads were excavated from the site.
Other finds include tomoe-shaped bronze implements, swords, magatama beads, and tubular beads. The human remains discovered in the tomb are believed to belong to a male.== See Also == * {{ill|Yamanohane No. 1 Kofun|ja|山ノ鼻1号墳}}

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name =丸隈山古墳 |image ={{image募集中|cat=福岡市}} |imageキャプション = |別名 = | part_of = {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|ja|今宿古墳群}} |所在地 ={{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}} {{ill|Fukuoka City|ja|福岡市}} {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|ja|西区}} {{ill|Susenji, Fukuoka|ja|周船寺}} |coordinates = |緯度度 =33 |緯度分 =34 |緯度秒 =25.3 |経度度 =130 |経度分 =15 |経度秒 =7.1 |ISO =JP-40 |type ={{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |規模 =Total length 84.6 m, rear circular section diameter 60 m, front section 25 m |埋葬施設 = |burial goods =Bronze mirrors, swords, iron arrowheads, etc. |陪塚 = |築造時期 =Early 5th century |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 =Designated as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] |有形文化財 = |埋蔵文化財包蔵地番号 = |特記事項 = |地図 =Japan Fukuoka |地図2 = |アイコン = |ラベル = |ラベルcoordinates = }} '''Marukumayama Kofun''' (丸隈山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located at the foot of {{ill|Takasu Mountain|ja|高祖山}} in {{ill|Susenji, Fukuoka|ja|周船寺}}, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|ja|西区}}, {{ill|Fukuoka City|ja|福岡市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}. It is designated as one of the key tumuli in the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|ja|今宿古墳群}}, which has been recognized as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].
== map_size and Features == The tumulus measures 84.6 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 60 meters and the front section measuring 25 meters. It is estimated to be the burial site of a chieftain from the early 5th century.
The kofun features a {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} made of basalt and an internal stone sarcophagus, which are open to public viewing at no cost. It is one of the few nationally designated tumuli where visitors can directly see the stone sarcophagus. According to the Edo period document {{ill|Chikuzen Koku Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記}} by {{ill|Kaibara Ekken|ja|貝原益軒}}, artifacts such as bronze mirrors, swords, and iron arrowheads were excavated from the site.
Other finds include tomoe-shaped bronze implements, swords, magatama beads, and tubular beads. The human remains discovered in the tomb are believed to belong to a male.== See Also == * {{ill|Yamanohane No. 1 Kofun|ja|山ノ鼻1号墳}}

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name =丸隈山古墳 |image ={{image募集中|cat=福岡市}} |imageキャプション = |別名 = | part_of = {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|ja|今宿古墳群}} |所在地 ={{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}} {{ill|Fukuoka City|ja|福岡市}} {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|ja|西区}} {{ill|Susenji, Fukuoka|ja|周船寺}} |coordinates = |緯度度 =33 |緯度分 =34 |緯度秒 =25.3 |経度度 =130 |経度分 =15 |経度秒 =7.1 |ISO =JP-40 |type ={{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |規模 =Total length 84.6 m, rear circular section diameter 60 m, front section 25 m |埋葬施設 = |burial goods =Bronze mirrors, swords, iron arrowheads, etc. |陪塚 = |築造時期 =Early 5th century |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 =Designated as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] |有形文化財 = |埋蔵文化財包蔵地番号 = |特記事項 = |地図 =Japan Fukuoka |地図2 = |アイコン = |ラベル = |ラベルcoordinates = }} '''Marukumayama Kofun''' (丸隈山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located at the foot of {{ill|Takasu Mountain|ja|高祖山}} in {{ill|Susenji, Fukuoka|ja|周船寺}}, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|ja|西区}}, {{ill|Fukuoka City|ja|福岡市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}. It is designated as one of the key tumuli in the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|ja|今宿古墳群}}, which has been recognized as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].
== map_size and Features == The tumulus measures 84.6 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 60 meters and the front section measuring 25 meters. It is estimated to be the burial site of a chieftain from the early 5th century.
The kofun features a {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} made of basalt and an internal stone sarcophagus, which are open to public viewing at no cost. It is one of the few nationally designated tumuli where visitors can directly see the stone sarcophagus. According to the Edo period document {{ill|Chikuzen Koku Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記}} by {{ill|Kaibara Ekken|ja|貝原益軒}}, artifacts such as bronze mirrors, swords, and iron arrowheads were excavated from the site.
Other finds include tomoe-shaped bronze implements, swords, magatama beads, and tubular beads. The human remains discovered in the tomb are believed to belong to a male.== See Also == * {{ill|Yamanohane No. 1 Kofun|ja|山ノ鼻1号墳}}
== External Links == * {{other_designationDatabase|401|2614|Imajuku Kofun Cluster}} * [https://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/cultural_properties/detail/item_id:101009 Imajuku Kofun Cluster (Marukumayama Kofun)] - Fukuoka City other_designation * [https://www.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/nishiku/c-shinko/charm/nisikunotakara/marukumayama_kofun.html Marukumayama Kofun] - Fukuoka City Homepage

The kofun features a {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} made of basalt and an internal stone sarcophagus, which are open to public viewing at no cost. It is one of the few nationally designated tumuli where visitors can directly see the stone sarcophagus. According to the Edo period document {{ill|Chikuzen Koku Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記}} by {{ill|Kaibara Ekken|ja|貝原益軒}}, artifacts such as bronze mirrors, swords, and iron arrowheads were excavated from the site.

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name =丸隈山古墳 |image ={{image募集中|cat=福岡市}} |imageキャプション = |別名 = | part_of = {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|ja|今宿古墳群}} |所在地 ={{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}} {{ill|Fukuoka City|ja|福岡市}} {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|ja|西区}} {{ill|Susenji, Fukuoka|ja|周船寺}} |coordinates = |緯度度 =33 |緯度分 =34 |緯度秒 =25.3 |経度度 =130 |経度分 =15 |経度秒 =7.1 |ISO =JP-40 |type ={{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |規模 =Total length 84.6 m, rear circular section diameter 60 m, front section 25 m |埋葬施設 = |burial goods =Bronze mirrors, swords, iron arrowheads, etc. |陪塚 = |築造時期 =Early 5th century |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 =Designated as a [[National other_designation of Japan]] |有形文化財 = |埋蔵文化財包蔵地番号 = |特記事項 = |地図 =Japan Fukuoka |地図2 = |アイコン = |ラベル = |ラベルcoordinates = }} '''Marukumayama Kofun''' (丸隈山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located at the foot of {{ill|Takasu Mountain|ja|高祖山}} in {{ill|Susenji, Fukuoka|ja|周船寺}}, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|ja|西区}}, {{ill|Fukuoka City|ja|福岡市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}. It is designated as one of the key tumuli in the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|ja|今宿古墳群}}, which has been recognized as a [[National other_designation of Japan]].
== map_size and Features == The tumulus measures 84.6 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 60 meters and the front section measuring 25 meters. It is estimated to be the burial site of a chieftain from the early 5th century.
The kofun features a {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} made of basalt and an internal stone sarcophagus, which are open to public viewing at no cost. It is one of the few nationally designated tumuli where visitors can directly see the stone sarcophagus. According to the Edo period document {{ill|Chikuzen Koku Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記}} by {{ill|Kaibara Ekken|ja|貝原益軒}}, artifacts such as bronze mirrors, swords, and iron arrowheads were excavated from the site.
Other finds include tomoe-shaped bronze implements, swords, magatama beads, and tubular beads. The human remains discovered in the tomb are believed to belong to a male.== See Also == * {{ill|Yamanohane No. 1 Kofun|ja|山ノ鼻1号墳}}
== External Links == * {{other_designationDatabase|401|2614|Imajuku Kofun Cluster}} * [https://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/cultural_properties/detail/item_id:101009 Imajuku Kofun Cluster (Marukumayama Kofun)] - Fukuoka City other_designation * [https://www.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/nishiku/c-shinko/charm/nisikunotakara/marukumayama_kofun.html Marukumayama Kofun] - Fukuoka City Homepage

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name =丸隈山古墳 |image ={{image募集中|cat=福岡市}} |imageキャプション = |別名 = | part_of = {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|ja|今宿古墳群}} |location ={{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}} {{ill|Fukuoka City|ja|福岡市}} {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|ja|西区}} {{ill|Susenji, Fukuoka|ja|周船寺}} |coordinates = |緯度度 =33 |緯度分 =34 |緯度秒 =25.3 |経度度 =130 |経度分 =15 |経度秒 =7.1 |omitted =JP-40 |type ={{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |area =Total length 84.6 m, rear circular section diameter 60 m, front section 25 m |埋葬施設 = |burial goods =Bronze mirrors, swords, iron arrowheads, etc. |陪塚 = |築造時期 =Early 5th century |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 =Designated as a [[National other_designation of Japan]] |有形文化財 = |埋蔵文化財包蔵地番号 = |特記事項 = |地図 =Japan Fukuoka |地図2 = |アイコン = |ラベル = |ラベルcoordinates = }} '''Marukumayama Kofun''' (丸隈山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located at the foot of {{ill|Takasu Mountain|ja|高祖山}} in {{ill|Susenji, Fukuoka|ja|周船寺}}, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|ja|西区}}, {{ill|Fukuoka City|ja|福岡市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}. It is designated as one of the key tumuli in the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|ja|今宿古墳群}}, which has been recognized as a [[National other_designation of Japan]].
== map_size and Features == The tumulus measures 84.6 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 60 meters and the front section measuring 25 meters. It is estimated to be the burial site of a chieftain from the early 5th century.
The kofun features a {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} made of basalt and an internal stone sarcophagus, which are open to public viewing at no cost. It is one of the few nationally designated tumuli where visitors can directly see the stone sarcophagus. According to the Edo period document {{ill|Chikuzen Koku Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記}} by {{ill|Kaibara Ekken|ja|貝原益軒}}, artifacts such as bronze mirrors, swords, and iron arrowheads were excavated from the site.
Other finds include tomoe-shaped bronze implements, swords, magatama beads, and tubular beads. The human remains discovered in the tomb are believed to belong to a male.== See Also == * {{ill|Yamanohane No. 1 Kofun|ja|山ノ鼻1号墳}}
== External Links == * {{other_designationDatabase|401|2614|Imajuku Kofun Cluster}} * [https://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/cultural_properties/detail/item_id:101009 Imajuku Kofun Cluster (Marukumayama Kofun)] - Fukuoka City other_designation * [https://www.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/nishiku/c-shinko/charm/nisikunotakara/marukumayama_kofun.html Marukumayama Kofun] - Fukuoka City Homepage

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name =丸隈山古墳 |image ={{image募集中|cat=福岡市}} |imageキャプション = |別名 = | part_of = {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|ja|今宿古墳群}} |location ={{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}} {{ill|Fukuoka City|ja|福岡市}} {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|ja|西区}} {{ill|Susenji, Fukuoka|ja|周船寺}} |coordinates = |緯度度 =33 |緯度分 =34 |緯度秒 =25.3 |経度度 =130 |経度分 =15 |経度秒 =7.1 |omitted =JP-40 |type ={{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |area =Total length 84.6 m, rear circular section diameter 60 m, front section 25 m |埋葬施設 = |burial goods =Bronze mirrors, swords, iron arrowheads, etc. |陪塚 = |築造時期 =Early 5th century |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 =Designated as a [[National other_other_designation of Japan]] |有形文化財 = |埋蔵文化財包蔵地番号 = |特記事項 = |地図 =Japan Fukuoka |地図2 = |アイコン = |ラベル = |ラベルcoordinates = }} '''Marukumayama Kofun''' (丸隈山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located at the foot of {{ill|Takasu Mountain|ja|高祖山}} in {{ill|Susenji, Fukuoka|ja|周船寺}}, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|ja|西区}}, {{ill|Fukuoka City|ja|福岡市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}. It is other_designation1 as one of the key tumuli in the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|ja|今宿古墳群}}, which has been recognized as a [[National other_other_designation of Japan]].
== map_size and Features == The tumulus measures 84.6 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 60 meters and the front section measuring 25 meters. It is estimated to be the burial site of a chieftain from the early 5th century.
The kofun features a {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} made of basalt and an internal stone sarcophagus, which are open to public viewing at no cost. It is one of the few nationally other_designation1 tumuli where visitors can directly see the stone sarcophagus. According to the Edo period document {{ill|Chikuzen Koku Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記}} by {{ill|Kaibara Ekken|ja|貝原益軒}}, burial goods such as bronze mirrors, swords, and iron arrowheads were excavated from the site.
Other finds include tomoe-shaped bronze implements, swords, magatama beads, and tubular beads. The human remains discovered in the tomb are believed to belong to a male.== See Also == * {{ill|Yamanohane No. 1 Kofun|ja|山ノ鼻1号墳}}

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name =丸隈山古墳 |image ={{image募集中|cat=福岡市}} |imageキャプション = |別名 = | part_of = {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|ja|今宿古墳群}} |location ={{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}} {{ill|Fukuoka City|ja|福岡市}} {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|ja|西区}} {{ill|Susenji, Fukuoka|ja|周船寺}} |coordinates = |緯度度 =33 |緯度分 =34 |緯度秒 =25.3 |経度度 =130 |経度分 =15 |経度秒 =7.1 |omitted =JP-40 |type ={{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |area =Total length 84.6 m, rear circular section diameter 60 m, front section 25 m |埋葬施設 = |burial goods =Bronze mirrors, swords, iron arrowheads, etc. |陪塚 = |築造時期 =Early 5th century |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 =Designated as a [[National other_designation of Japan]] |有形文化財 = |埋蔵文化財包蔵地番号 = |特記事項 = |地図 =Japan Fukuoka |地図2 = |アイコン = |ラベル = |ラベルcoordinates = }} '''Marukumayama Kofun''' (丸隈山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located at the foot of {{ill|Takasu Mountain|ja|高祖山}} in {{ill|Susenji, Fukuoka|ja|周船寺}}, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|ja|西区}}, {{ill|Fukuoka City|ja|福岡市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}. It is other_designation1 as one of the key tumuli in the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|ja|今宿古墳群}}, which has been recognized as a [[National other_designation of Japan]].
== map_size and Features == The tumulus measures 84.6 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 60 meters and the front section measuring 25 meters. It is estimated to be the burial site of a chieftain from the early 5th century.
The kofun features a {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} made of basalt and an internal stone sarcophagus, which are open to public viewing at no cost. It is one of the few nationally other_designation1 tumuli where visitors can directly see the stone sarcophagus. According to the Edo period document {{ill|Chikuzen Koku Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記}} by {{ill|Kaibara Ekken|ja|貝原益軒}}, burial goods such as bronze mirrors, swords, and iron arrowheads were excavated from the site.
Other finds include tomoe-shaped bronze implements, swords, magatama beads, and tubular beads. The human remains discovered in the tomb are believed to belong to a male.== See Also == * {{ill|Yamanohane No. 1 Kofun|ja|山ノ鼻1号墳}}
== External Links == * {{other_designationDatabase|401|2614|Imajuku Kofun Cluster}} * [https://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/cultural_properties/detail/item_id:101009 Imajuku Kofun Cluster (Marukumayama Kofun)] - Fukuoka City other_designation * [https://www.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/nishiku/c-shinko/charm/nisikunotakara/marukumayama_kofun.html Marukumayama Kofun] - Fukuoka City Homepage

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name =丸隈山古墳 |image ={{image募集中|cat=福岡市}} |imageキャプション = |別名 = | part_of = {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|ja|今宿古墳群}} |location ={{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}} {{ill|Fukuoka City|ja|福岡市}} {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|ja|西区}} {{ill|Susenji, Fukuoka|ja|周船寺}} |coordinates = |緯度度 =33 |緯度分 =34 |緯度秒 =25.3 |経度度 =130 |経度分 =15 |経度秒 =7.1 |omitted =JP-40 |type ={{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |area =Total length 84.6 m, rear circular section diameter 60 m, front section 25 m |埋葬施設 = |burial goods =Bronze mirrors, swords, iron arrowheads, etc. |陪塚 = |築造時期 =Early 5th century |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 =Designated as a [[National other_designation of Japan]] |有形文化財 = |埋蔵文化財包蔵地番号 = |特記事項 = |地図 =Japan Fukuoka |地図2 = |アイコン = |ラベル = |ラベルcoordinates = }} '''Marukumayama Kofun''' (丸隈山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located at the foot of {{ill|Takasu Mountain|ja|高祖山}} in {{ill|Susenji, Fukuoka|ja|周船寺}}, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|ja|西区}}, {{ill|Fukuoka City|ja|福岡市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}. It is other_designation1 as one of the key tumuli in the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|ja|今宿古墳群}}, which has been recognized as a [[National other_designation of Japan]].
== map_size and Features == The tumulus measures 84.6 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 60 meters and the front section measuring 25 meters. It is estimated to be the burial site of a chieftain from the early 5th century.
The kofun features a {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} made of basalt and an internal stone sarcophagus, which are open to public viewing at no cost. It is one of the few nationally other_designation1 tumuli where visitors can directly see the stone sarcophagus. According to the Edo period document {{ill|Chikuzen Koku Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記}} by {{ill|Kaibara Ekken|ja|貝原益軒}}, burial goods such as bronze mirrors, swords, and iron arrowheads were excavated from the site.
Other finds include tomoe-shaped bronze implements, swords, magatama beads, and tubular beads. The human remains discovered in the tomb are believed to belong to a male.== See Also == * {{ill|Yamanohane No. 1 Kofun|ja|山ノ鼻1号墳}}
== External Links == * {{other_designationDatabase|401|2614|Imajuku Kofun Cluster}} * [https://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/cultural_properties/detail/item_id:101009 Imajuku Kofun Cluster (Marukumayama Kofun)] - Fukuoka City other_designation * [https://www.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/nishiku/c-shinko/charm/nisikunotakara/marukumayama_kofun.html Marukumayama Kofun] - Fukuoka City Homepage

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name =丸隈山古墳 |image ={{image募集中|cat=福岡市}} |imageキャプション = |別名 = | part_of = {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|ja|今宿古墳群}} |location ={{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}} {{ill|Fukuoka City|ja|福岡市}} {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|ja|西区}} {{ill|Susenji, Fukuoka|ja|周船寺}} |coordinates = |緯度度 =33 |緯度分 =34 |緯度秒 =25.3 |経度度 =130 |経度分 =15 |経度秒 =7.1 |omitted =JP-40 |type ={{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |area =Total length 84.6 m, rear circular section diameter 60 m, front section 25 m |埋葬施設 = |burial goods =Bronze mirrors, swords, iron arrowheads, etc. |陪塚 = |築造時期 =Early 5th century |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 =Designated as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] |有形文化財 = |埋蔵文化財包蔵地番号 = |特記事項 = |地図 =Japan Fukuoka |地図2 = |アイコン = |ラベル = |ラベルcoordinates = }} '''Marukumayama Kofun''' (丸隈山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located at the foot of {{ill|Takasu Mountain|ja|高祖山}} in {{ill|Susenji, Fukuoka|ja|周船寺}}, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|ja|西区}}, {{ill|Fukuoka City|ja|福岡市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}. It is other_designation1 as one of the key tumuli in the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|ja|今宿古墳群}}, which has been recognized as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].
== map_size and Features == The tumulus measures 84.6 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 60 meters and the front section measuring 25 meters. It is estimated to be the burial site of a chieftain from the early 5th century.
The kofun features a {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} made of basalt and an internal stone sarcophagus, which are open to public viewing at no cost. It is one of the few nationally other_designation1 tumuli where visitors can directly see the stone sarcophagus. According to the Edo period document {{ill|Chikuzen Koku Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記}} by {{ill|Kaibara Ekken|ja|貝原益軒}}, burial goods such as bronze mirrors, swords, and iron arrowheads were excavated from the site.
Other finds include tomoe-shaped bronze implements, swords, magatama beads, and tubular beads. The human remains discovered in the tomb are believed to belong to a male.== See Also == * {{ill|Yamanohane No. 1 Kofun|ja|山ノ鼻1号墳}}

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name =丸隈山古墳 |image ={{image募集中|cat=福岡市}} |imageキャプション = |別名 = | part_of = {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|ja|今宿古墳群}} |location ={{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}} {{ill|Fukuoka City|ja|福岡市}} {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|ja|西区}} {{ill|Susenji, Fukuoka|ja|周船寺}} |coordinates = |緯度度 =33 |緯度分 =34 |緯度秒 =25.3 |経度度 =130 |経度分 =15 |経度秒 =7.1 |omitted =JP-40 |type ={{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |area =Total length 84.6 m, rear circular section diameter 60 m, front section 25 m |埋葬施設 = |burial goods =Bronze mirrors, swords, iron arrowheads, etc. |陪塚 = |築造時期 =Early 5th century |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 =Designated as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] |有形文化財 = |埋蔵文化財包蔵地番号 = |特記事項 = |地図 =Japan Fukuoka |地図2 = |アイコン = |ラベル = |ラベルcoordinates = }} '''Marukumayama Kofun''' (丸隈山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located at the foot of {{ill|Takasu Mountain|ja|高祖山}} in {{ill|Susenji, Fukuoka|ja|周船寺}}, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|ja|西区}}, {{ill|Fukuoka City|ja|福岡市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}. It is Historic Site1 as one of the key tumuli in the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|ja|今宿古墳群}}, which has been recognized as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].
== map_size and Features == The tumulus measures 84.6 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 60 meters and the front section measuring 25 meters. It is estimated to be the burial site of a chieftain from the early 5th century.
The kofun features a {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} made of basalt and an internal stone sarcophagus, which are open to public viewing at no cost. It is one of the few nationally Historic Site1 tumuli where visitors can directly see the stone sarcophagus. According to the Edo period document {{ill|Chikuzen Koku Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記}} by {{ill|Kaibara Ekken|ja|貝原益軒}}, burial goods such as bronze mirrors, swords, and iron arrowheads were excavated from the site.
Other finds include tomoe-shaped bronze implements, swords, magatama beads, and tubular beads. The human remains discovered in the tomb are believed to belong to a male.== See Also == * {{ill|Yamanohane No. 1 Kofun|ja|山ノ鼻1号墳}}

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name =丸隈山古墳 |image ={{image募集中|cat=福岡市}} |imageキャプション = |別名 = | part_of = {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|ja|今宿古墳群}} |location ={{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}} {{ill|Fukuoka City|ja|福岡市}} {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|ja|西区}} {{ill|Susenji, Fukuoka|ja|周船寺}} |coordinates = |緯度度 =33 |緯度分 =34 |緯度秒 =25.3 |経度度 =130 |経度分 =15 |経度秒 =7.1 |omitted =JP-40 |type ={{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |area =Total length 84.6 m, rear circular section diameter 60 m, front section 25 m |埋葬施設 = |burial goods =Bronze mirrors, swords, iron arrowheads, etc. |陪塚 = |築造時期 =Early 5th century |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 =Designated as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] |有形文化財 = |埋蔵文化財包蔵地番号 = |特記事項 = |地図 =Japan Fukuoka |地図2 = |アイコン = |ラベル = |ラベルcoordinates = }} '''Marukumayama Kofun''' (丸隈山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located at the foot of {{ill|Takasu Mountain|ja|高祖山}} in {{ill|Susenji, Fukuoka|ja|周船寺}}, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|ja|西区}}, {{ill|Fukuoka City|ja|福岡市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}. It is Historic Site1 as one of the key tumuli in the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|ja|今宿古墳群}}, which has been recognized as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].
== map_size and Features == The tumulus measures 84.6 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 60 meters and the front section measuring 25 meters. It is estimated to be the burial site of a chieftain from the early 5th century.
The kofun features a {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} made of basalt and an internal stone sarcophagus, which are open to public viewing at no cost. It is one of the few nationally Historic Site1 tumuli where visitors can directly see the stone sarcophagus. According to the Edo period document {{ill|Chikuzen Koku Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記}} by {{ill|Kaibara Ekken|ja|貝原益軒}}, burial goods such as bronze mirrors, swords, and iron arrowheads were excavated from the site.
Other finds include tomoe-shaped bronze implements, swords, magatama beads, and tubular beads. The human remains discovered in the tomb are believed to belong to a male.== See Also == * {{ill|Yamanohane No. 1 Kofun|ja|山ノ鼻1号墳}}
== External Links == * {{Historic SiteDatabase|401|2614|Imajuku Kofun Cluster}} * [https://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/cultural_properties/detail/item_id:101009 Imajuku Kofun Cluster (Marukumayama Kofun)] - Fukuoka City Historic Site * [https://www.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/nishiku/c-shinko/charm/nisikunotakara/marukumayama_kofun.html Marukumayama Kofun] - Fukuoka City Homepage

revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 =丸隈山古墳 |画像 ={{画像募集中|cat=福岡市}} |画像キャプション = |別名 = |所属 = [[今宿古墳群]] |所在地 =[[福岡県]][[福岡市]][[西区 (福岡市)|西区]][[周船寺]] |位置 = |緯度度 =33 |緯度分 =34 |緯度秒 =25.3 |経度度 =130 |経度分 =15 |経度秒 =7.1 |ISO =JP-40 |形状 =[[前方後円墳]] |規模 =全長84.6m、後円部直径60m、前方部25m |埋葬施設 = |出土品 =銅鏡、刀、鉄鏃など |陪塚 = |築造時期 =[[5世紀]]前半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 =国指定史跡 |有形文化財 = |埋蔵文化財包蔵地番号 = |特記事項 = |地図 =Japan Fukuoka |地図2 = |アイコン = |ラベル = |ラベル位置 = }} '''丸隈山古墳'''（まるくまやまこふん）は、[[福岡県]][[福岡市]][[西区 (福岡市)|西区]][[周船寺]]の[[高祖山]]のふもとに位置する[[前方後円墳]]である。「[[今宿古墳群]]」のうちの1基として国の[[史跡]]に指定されている。
== 規模等 == 全長84.6m、後円部の直径60m、前方部25m。5世紀前半の首長級の墳墓と推定されている。玄武岩で作られた横穴式石室と内部の石棺は無料公開されている。国の史跡に指定されている古墳のうち、見学者が石棺を直接見ることができる数少ないものの1つである。江戸時代の「[[筑前国続風土記]]」で[[貝原益軒]]は、ここから銅鏡、刀、鉄鏃などが出土したと記録している。 その他に巴形銅器、剣、勾玉、管玉なども出土した。埋葬人骨は男性のものとされている。
== 関連項目 == *[[山ノ鼻1号墳]]
== 外部リンク == *{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|2614|今宿古墳群}} *[https://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/cultural_properties/detail/item_id:101009 今宿古墳群（丸隈山古墳）] - 福岡市の文化財 *[https://www.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/nishiku/c-shinko/charm/nisikunotakara/marukumayama_kofun.html 丸隈山古墳] - 福岡市ホームページ
{{DEFAULTSORT:まるくまやまこふん}} [[Category:福岡県の古墳]] [[Category:福岡県にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:福岡市西区の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] {{Japanese-history-stub}}

revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 =丸隈山古墳 |画像 ={{画像募集中|cat=福岡市}} |画像キャプション = |別名 = |所属 = [[今宿古墳群]] |所在地 =[[福岡県]][[福岡市]][[西区 (福岡市)|西区]][[周船寺]] |位置 = |緯度度 =33 |緯度分 =34 |緯度秒 =25.3 |経度度 =130 |経度分 =15 |経度秒 =7.1 |ISO =JP-40 |形状 =[[前方後円墳]] |規模 =全長84.6m、後円部直径60m、前方部25m |埋葬施設 = |出土品 =銅鏡、刀、鉄鏃など |陪塚 = |築造時期 =[[5世紀]]前半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 =国指定史跡 |有形文化財 = |埋蔵文化財包蔵地番号 = |特記事項 = |地図 =Japan Fukuoka |地図2 = |アイコン = |ラベル = |ラベル位置 = }} '''丸隈山古墳'''（まるくまやまこふん）は、[[福岡県]][[福岡市]][[西区 (福岡市)|西区]][[周船寺]]の[[高祖山]]のふもとに位置する[[前方後円墳]]である。「[[今宿古墳群]]」のうちの1基として国の[[史跡]]に指定されている。
== 規模等 == 全長84.6m、後円部の直径60m、前方部25m。5世紀前半の首長級の墳墓と推定されている。玄武岩で作られた横穴式石室と内部の石棺は無料公開されている。国の史跡に指定されている古墳のうち、見学者が石棺を直接見ることができる数少ないものの1つである。江戸時代の「[[筑前国続風土記]]」で[[貝原益軒]]は、ここから銅鏡、刀、鉄鏃などが出土したと記録している。 その他に巴形銅器、剣、勾玉、管玉なども出土した。埋葬人骨は男性のものとされている。
== 関連項目 == *[[山ノ鼻1号墳]]
== 外部リンク == *{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|2614|今宿古墳群}} *[https://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/cultural_properties/detail/item_id:101009 今宿古墳群（丸隈山古墳）] - 福岡市の文化財 *[https://www.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/nishiku/c-shinko/charm/nisikunotakara/marukumayama_kofun.html 丸隈山古墳] - 福岡市ホームページ
{{DEFAULTSORT:まるくまやまこふん}} [[Category:福岡県の古墳]] [[Category:福岡県にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:福岡市西区の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] {{Japanese-history-stub}}

'''Marukumayama Kofun''' (丸隈山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located at the foot of {{ill|Mount Takasu|lt=Mount Takasu|lt=Takasu Mountain|ja|高祖山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasu Mountain|qq=}} in {{ill|Susenji, Fukuoka|ja|周船寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susenji, Fukuoka|qq=|1=Susenji, Fukuoka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109600963}}, [[:en:Nishi-ku|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka]], [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka City]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It is Historic Site1 as one of the key tumuli in the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|ja|今宿古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imajuku Kofun Cluster|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which has been recognized as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].

== map_size and Features ==
The tumulus measures 84.6 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 60 meters and the front section measuring 25 meters. It is estimated to be the burial site of a chieftain from the early 5th century. 

The kofun features a {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} made of basalt and an internal stone sarcophagus, which are open to public viewing at no cost. It is one of the few nationally Historic Site1 tumuli where visitors can directly see the stone sarcophagus. According to the Edo period document {{ill|Chikuzen no Kuni Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuzen Koku Zoku Fudoki|qq=}} by [[:en:Kaibara Ekken|Kaibara Ekken]], burial goods such as bronze mirrors, swords, and iron arrowheads were excavated from the site. 

Other finds include tomoe-shaped bronze implements, swords, magatama beads, and tubular beads. The human remains discovered in the tomb are believed to belong to a male.
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Yamanohane No. 1 Kofun|ja|山ノ鼻1号墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanohane No. 1 Kofun|qq=}}

== External Links ==
* {{Historic SiteDatabase|401|2614|Imajuku Kofun Cluster}}
* [https://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/cultural_properties/detail/item_id:101009 Imajuku Kofun Cluster (Marukumayama Kofun)] - Fukuoka City Historic Site
* [https://www.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/nishiku/c-shinko/charm/nisikunotakara/marukumayama_kofun.html Marukumayama Kofun] - Fukuoka City Homepage

The kofun features a {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} made of basalt and an internal stone sarcophagus, which are open to public viewing at no cost. It is one of the few nationally Historic Site1 tumuli where visitors can directly see the stone sarcophagus. According to the Edo period document {{ill|Chikuzen no Kuni Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuzen Koku Zoku Fudoki|qq=}} by [[:en:Kaibara Ekken|Kaibara Ekken]], burial goods such as bronze mirrors, swords, and iron arrowheads were excavated from the site. 

Other finds include tomoe-shaped bronze implements, swords, magatama beads, and tubular beads. The human remains discovered in the tomb are believed to belong to a male.

'''Marukumayama Kofun''' (丸隈山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located at the foot of {{ill|Mount Takasu|lt=Mount Takasu|lt=Takasu Mountain|ja|高祖山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasu Mountain|qq=}} in {{ill|Susenji, Fukuoka|ja|周船寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susenji, Fukuoka|1=Susenji, Fukuoka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109600963}}, [[:en:Nishi-ku|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka]], [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka City]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It is Historic Site1 as one of the key tumuli in the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|ja|今宿古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imajuku Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which has been recognized as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].

== map_size and Features ==
The tumulus measures 84.6 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 60 meters and the front section measuring 25 meters. It is estimated to be the burial site of a chieftain from the early 5th century. 

The kofun features a {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} made of basalt and an internal stone sarcophagus, which are open to public viewing at no cost. It is one of the few nationally Historic Site1 tumuli where visitors can directly see the stone sarcophagus. According to the Edo period document {{ill|Chikuzen no Kuni Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuzen Koku Zoku Fudoki|qq=}} by [[:en:Kaibara Ekken|Kaibara Ekken]], burial goods such as bronze mirrors, swords, and iron arrowheads were excavated from the site. 

Other finds include tomoe-shaped bronze implements, swords, magatama beads, and tubular beads. The human remains discovered in the tomb are believed to belong to a male.
== See Also ==

'''Marukumayama Kofun''' (丸隈山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located at the foot of {{ill|Mount Takasu|lt=Mount Takasu|lt=Takasu Mountain|ja|高祖山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasu Mountain|qq=}} in {{ill|Susenji, Fukuoka|ja|周船寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susenji, Fukuoka|1=Susenji, Fukuoka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109600963}}, [[:en:Nishi-ku|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka]], [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka City]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It is Historic Site1 as one of the key tumuli in the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|ja|今宿古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imajuku Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which has been recognized as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].

== map_size and Features ==
The tumulus measures 84.6 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 60 meters and the front section measuring 25 meters. It is estimated to be the burial site of a chieftain from the early 5th century. 

The kofun features a {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} made of basalt and an internal stone sarcophagus, which are open to public viewing at no cost. It is one of the few nationally Historic Site1 tumuli where visitors can directly see the stone sarcophagus. According to the Edo period document {{ill|Chikuzen no Kuni Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuzen Koku Zoku Fudoki|qq=}} by [[:en:Kaibara Ekken|Kaibara Ekken]], burial goods such as bronze mirrors, swords, and iron arrowheads were excavated from the site. 

Other finds include tomoe-shaped bronze implements, swords, magatama beads, and tubular beads. The human remains discovered in the tomb are believed to belong to a male.
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Yamanohane No. 1 Kofun|ja|山ノ鼻1号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanohane No. 1 Kofun|qq=}}

According to the Edo period document {{ill|Chikuzen no Kuni Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuzen Koku Zoku Fudoki|qq=}} by [[:en:Kaibara Ekken|Kaibara Ekken]], burial goods such as bronze mirrors, swords, and iron arrowheads were excavated from the site. 

Other finds include tomoe-shaped bronze implements, swords, magatama beads, and tubular beads. The human remains discovered in the tomb are believed to belong to a male.

'''Marukumayama Kofun''' (丸隈山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located at the foot of {{ill|Mount Takasu|lt=Mount Takasu|lt=Takasu Mountain|ja|高祖山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasu Mountain|qq=}} in {{ill|Susenji, Fukuoka|ja|周船寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susenji, Fukuoka|1=Susenji, Fukuoka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109600963}}, [[:en:Nishi-ku|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka]], [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka City]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It is Historic Site1 as one of the key tumuli in the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|ja|今宿古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imajuku Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which has been recognized as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].

== map_size and Features ==
The tumulus measures 84.6 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 60 meters and the front section measuring 25 meters. It is estimated to be the burial site of a chieftain from the early 5th century. 

The kofun features a {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} made of basalt and an internal stone sarcophagus, which are open to public viewing at no cost. It is one of the few nationally Historic Site1 tumuli where visitors can directly see the stone sarcophagus. According to the Edo period document {{ill|Chikuzen no Kuni Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuzen Koku Zoku Fudoki|qq=}} by [[:en:Kaibara Ekken|Kaibara Ekken]], burial goods such as bronze mirrors, swords, and iron arrowheads were excavated from the site. 

Other finds include tomoe-shaped bronze implements, swords, magatama beads, and tubular beads. The human remains discovered in the tomb are believed to belong to a male.
== See Also ==

'''Marukumayama Kofun''' (丸隈山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located at the foot of {{ill|Mount Takasu|lt=Mount Takasu|lt=Takasu Mountain|ja|高祖山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasu Mountain|qq=}} in {{ill|Susenji, Fukuoka|ja|周船寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susenji, Fukuoka|1=Susenji, Fukuoka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109600963}}, [[:en:Nishi-ku|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka]], [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka City]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It is Historic Site1 as one of the key tumuli in the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|ja|今宿古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imajuku Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which has been recognized as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].

== map_size and Features ==
The tumulus measures 84.6 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 60 meters and the front section measuring 25 meters. It is estimated to be the burial site of a chieftain from the early 5th century. 

The kofun features a {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} made of basalt and an internal stone sarcophagus, which are open to public viewing at no cost. It is one of the few nationally Historic Site1 tumuli where visitors can directly see the stone sarcophagus. According to the Edo period document {{ill|Chikuzen no Kuni Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuzen Koku Zoku Fudoki|qq=}} by [[:en:Kaibara Ekken|Kaibara Ekken]], burial goods such as bronze mirrors, swords, and iron arrowheads were excavated from the site. 

Other finds include tomoe-shaped bronze implements, swords, magatama beads, and tubular beads. The human remains discovered in the tomb are believed to belong to a male.
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Yamanohane No. 1 Kofun|ja|山ノ鼻1号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanohane No. 1 Kofun|qq=}}

== External Links ==

* [https://www.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/nishiku/c-shinko/charm/nisikunotakara/marukumayama_kofun.html Marukumayama Kofun] - Fukuoka City Homepage
