The tumulus measures 84.6 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 60 meters and the front section measuring 25 meters. It is estimated to be the burial site of a chieftain from the early 5th century. 

The kofun features a {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} made of basalt and an internal stone sarcophagus, which are open to public viewing at no cost. It is one of the few nationally Historic Site1 tumuli where visitors can directly see the stone sarcophagus. According to the Edo period document {{ill|Chikuzen no Kuni Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuzen Koku Zoku Fudoki|qq=}} by [[:en:Kaibara Ekken|Kaibara Ekken]], burial goods such as bronze mirrors, swords, and iron arrowheads were excavated from the site. 

Other finds include tomoe-shaped bronze implements, swords, magatama beads, and tubular beads. The human remains discovered in the tomb are believed to belong to a male.
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Yamanohane No. 1 Kofun|ja|山ノ鼻1号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanohane No. 1 Kofun|qq=}}

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name =丸隈山古墳 |image ={{image requested|cat=福岡市}} |imageキャプション = |alternate_name = | part_of = {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Group|WD=Q103946619}} |location ={{ill|Fukuoka|WD=Q26600}} |coordinates ={{Coord|33.573694|N|130.251972|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |omitted =JP-40 |type ={{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} |area =Total length 84.6 m, rear circular section diameter 60 m, front section 25 m |Burial Facility = |burial goods =Bronze mirrors, swords, iron arrowheads, etc. |陪塚 = |built =Early 5th century |Interred Persons = |陵墓 = |史跡 =Designated as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] |有形文化財 = |埋蔵文化財包蔵地番号 = |Special Notes = |地図 =Japan Fukuoka |地図2 = |アイコン = |ラベル = |ラベルcoordinates = |architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Marukumayama Kofun''' (丸隈山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} located at the foot of {{ill|Mount Takasu|lt=Mount Takasu|lt=Takasu Mountain|ja|高祖山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasu Mountain|qq=}} in {{ill|Susenji, Fukuoka|ja|周船寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susenji, Fukuoka|1=Susenji, Fukuoka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109600963}}, {{ill|Nishi-ku|en|Nishi-ku|WD=Q296095|lt=Nishi-ku, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Fukuoka|en|Fukuoka|WD=Q26600|lt=Fukuoka City}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}}. It is Historic Site1 as one of the key tumuli in the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|ja|今宿古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imajuku Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which has been recognized as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].
== map_size and Features == The tumulus measures 84.6 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 60 meters and the front section measuring 25 meters. It is estimated to be the burial site of a chieftain from the early 5th century.
The kofun features a {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} made of basalt and an internal stone sarcophagus, which are open to public viewing at no cost. It is one of the few nationally Historic Site1 tumuli where visitors can directly see the stone sarcophagus. According to the Edo period document {{ill|Chikuzen no Kuni Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuzen Koku Zoku Fudoki|qq=}} by {{ill|Kaibara Ekken|en|Kaibara Ekken|WD=Q1721396|lt=Kaibara Ekken}}, burial goods such as bronze mirrors, swords, and iron arrowheads were excavated from the site.
Other finds include tomoe-shaped bronze implements, swords, magatama beads, and tubular beads. The human remains discovered in the tomb are believed to belong to a male. == See Also == * {{ill|Yamanohane No. 1 Kofun|ja|山ノ鼻1号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanohane No. 1 Kofun|qq=}}
== External Links == * {{Historic SiteDatabase|401|2614|Imajuku Kofun Cluster}} * [https://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/cultural_properties/detail/item_id:101009 Imajuku Kofun Cluster (Marukumayama Kofun)] - Fukuoka City Historic Site * [https://www.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/nishiku/c-shinko/charm/nisikunotakara/marukumayama_kofun.html Marukumayama Kofun] - Fukuoka City Homepage

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name =丸隈山古墳 |image ={{image requested|cat=福岡市}} |imageキャプション = |alternate_name = | part_of = {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Group|WD=Q103946619|1=Imajuku Kofun Cluster}} |location ={{ill|Fukuoka|WD=Q26600|1=Fukuoka}} |coordinates ={{Coord|33.573694|N|130.251972|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |omitted =JP-40 |type ={{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} |area =Total length 84.6 m, rear circular section diameter 60 m, front section 25 m |Burial Facility = |burial goods =Bronze mirrors, swords, iron arrowheads, etc. |陪塚 = |built =Early 5th century |Interred Persons = |陵墓 = |史跡 =Designated as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] |有形文化財 = |埋蔵文化財包蔵地番号 = |Special Notes = |地図 =Japan Fukuoka |地図2 = |アイコン = |ラベル = |ラベルcoordinates = |architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Marukumayama Kofun''' (丸隈山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} located at the foot of {{ill|Mount Takasu|lt=Mount Takasu|lt=Takasu Mountain|ja|高祖山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasu Mountain|qq=}} in {{ill|Susenji, Fukuoka|ja|周船寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susenji, Fukuoka|1=Q109600963|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109600963}}, {{ill|Nishi-ku|en|Nishi-ku|WD=Q296095|lt=Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|1=Nishi-ku}}, {{ill|Fukuoka|en|Fukuoka|WD=Q26600|lt=Fukuoka City|1=Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}. It is Historic Site1 as one of the key tumuli in the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|ja|今宿古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imajuku Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which has been recognized as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].
== map_size and Features == The tumulus measures 84.6 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 60 meters and the front section measuring 25 meters. It is estimated to be the burial site of a chieftain from the early 5th century.
The kofun features a {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} made of basalt and an internal stone sarcophagus, which are open to public viewing at no cost. It is one of the few nationally Historic Site1 tumuli where visitors can directly see the stone sarcophagus. According to the Edo period document {{ill|Chikuzen no Kuni Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuzen Koku Zoku Fudoki|qq=}} by {{ill|Kaibara Ekken|en|Kaibara Ekken|WD=Q1721396|lt=Kaibara Ekken|1=Kaibara Ekken}}, burial goods such as bronze mirrors, swords, and iron arrowheads were excavated from the site.
Other finds include tomoe-shaped bronze implements, swords, magatama beads, and tubular beads. The human remains discovered in the tomb are believed to belong to a male.
== See Also == * {{ill|Yamanohane No. 1 Kofun|ja|山ノ鼻1号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanohane No. 1 Kofun|qq=}}
== External Links == * {{Historic SiteDatabase|401|2614|Imajuku Kofun Cluster}} * [https://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/cultural_properties/detail/item_id:101009 Imajuku Kofun Cluster (Marukumayama Kofun)] - Fukuoka City Historic Site * [https://www.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/nishiku/c-shinko/charm/nisikunotakara/marukumayama_kofun.html Marukumayama Kofun] - Fukuoka City Homepage

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpōkōenfun]] |name =丸隈山古墳 |image ={{image requested|cat=福岡市}} |imageキャプション = |alternate_name = | part_of = {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Group|qid=Q103946619|1=Imajuku Kofun Cluster}} |location ={{ill|Fukuoka|qid=Q26600|1=Fukuoka}} |coordinates ={{Coord|33.573694|N|130.251972|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |omitted =JP-40 |type ={{ill|Zenpōkōenfun|en|Zenpōkōenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpōkōenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} |area =Total length 84.6 m, rear circular section diameter 60 m, front section 25 m |Burial Facility = |burial goods =Bronze mirrors, swords, iron arrowheads, etc. |陪塚 = |built =Early 5th century |Interred Persons = |陵墓 = |史跡 =Designated as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] |有形文化財 = |埋蔵文化財包蔵地番号 = |Special Notes = |地図 =Japan Fukuoka |地図2 = |アイコン = |ラベル = |ラベルcoordinates = |architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Marukumayama Kofun''' (丸隈山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpōkōenfun|en|Zenpōkōenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpōkōenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} located at the foot of {{ill|Mount Takasu|lt=Mount Takasu|lt=Takasu Mountain|ja|高祖山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasu Mountain|qq=}} in {{ill|Susenji, Fukuoka|ja|周船寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susenji, Fukuoka|1=Q109600963|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q109600963}}, {{ill|Nishi-ku|en|Nishi-ku|qid=Q296095|lt=Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|1=Nishi-ku}}, {{ill|Fukuoka|en|Fukuoka|qid=Q26600|lt=Fukuoka City|1=Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|qid=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}. It is Historic Site1 as one of the key tumuli in the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|ja|今宿古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imajuku Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which has been recognized as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].
== map_size and Features == The tumulus measures 84.6 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 60 meters and the front section measuring 25 meters. It is estimated to be the burial site of a chieftain from the early 5th century.
The kofun features a {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} made of basalt and an internal stone sarcophagus, which are open to public viewing at no cost. It is one of the few nationally Historic Site1 tumuli where visitors can directly see the stone sarcophagus. According to the Edo period document {{ill|Chikuzen no Kuni Zoku Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuzen Koku Zoku Fudoki|qq=}} by {{ill|Kaibara Ekken|en|Kaibara Ekken|qid=Q1721396|lt=Kaibara Ekken|1=Kaibara Ekken}}, burial goods such as bronze mirrors, swords, and iron arrowheads were excavated from the site.
Other finds include tomoe-shaped bronze implements, swords, magatama beads, and tubular beads. The human remains discovered in the tomb are believed to belong to a male.
== See Also == * {{ill|Yamanohane No. 1 Kofun|ja|山ノ鼻1号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanohane No. 1 Kofun|qq=}}
== External Links == * {{Historic SiteDatabase|401|2614|Imajuku Kofun Cluster}} * [https://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/cultural_properties/detail/item_id:101009 Imajuku Kofun Cluster (Marukumayama Kofun)] - Fukuoka City Historic Site * [https://www.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/nishiku/c-shinko/charm/nisikunotakara/marukumayama_kofun.html Marukumayama Kofun] - Fukuoka City Homepage
